---
title: "Figure 3 — First-order diagnostics: the classical methods pass the obvious checks"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
PUBLICATION figure 3. The "alibi": at the level of data fit and first-order
statistics every algorithm looks fine, which is exactly why a covariance-level
error went unnoticed. Three null-zero checks:

  A  residual mean      Moment_statistics_Sum_r_std        (null 0)
  B  expected score     Moment_statistics_Sum_dlogL @ sim  (null 0, per parameter)
  C  estimator bias     theta_bar - theta_sim              (null 0, bootstrap CI)

Idioms (read skip=1, component strip, boot_param_probit, theta_sim_of) are taken
verbatim from figure_3_paper.Rmd.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
DATA_DIR  <- "../data/433ed13"
NSIM      <- 10000         # use the 10000-replica runs, NOT the nsim_1000 ones
NOISE     <- "0.1"
NCH       <- c(10, 100, 1000, 10000)
ALGOS     <- c("macro_NR","macro_NMR","macro_R","macro_MR","macro_IR")
ALGO_LAB  <- c(macro_NR="NR", macro_NMR="NMR", macro_R="R", macro_MR="MR", macro_IR="IR")
ALGO_COL  <- c(NR="#D55E00", NMR="#E69F00", R="#CC79A7", MR="#0072B2", IR="#009E73")
BIAS_GROUP <- 10           # group_size used for the bias panel cloud
KIN       <- c("on","off") # kinetic parameters for the score panel (B)
PARAMS_C  <- c("on","off","unitary_current") # bias panel (C): + unitary_current,
                           # which shows the non-monotonic N_ch bias from non-normality
```

```{r read}
read_skip1 <- function(path) read.csv(path, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

grid  <- expand.grid(nch = NCH, algo = ALGOS, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
base  <- file.path(DATA_DIR, sprintf("figure_3_nch_%d_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s",
                                     grid$nch, NSIM, grid$algo, NOISE))
base  <- base[file.exists(paste0(base, "_battery_sim.csv"))]

d <- bind_rows(
  map_dfr(paste0(base, "_battery_sim.csv"),    read_skip1),
  map_dfr(paste0(base, "_mle_cloud_runs.csv"), read_skip1)
) %>%
  select(where(~ !all(is.na(.)))) %>%
  mutate(component = str_replace(component_path, "^Probit_statistics_", ""),
         algo = factor(ALGO_LAB[algorithm], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)))
```

```{r d_mean}
# wide over the bootstrap summary (mean_NA + quantile_*), like figure_3_paper.Rmd
d_mean <- d %>%
  filter(!component %in% c("Probit_statistics")) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c(probit, quantile_level), values_from = value)
```

```{r panelA-residuals, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 5}
# Residual diagnostics CORRECTED PER MEASUREMENT: the stored quantity is a SUM over
# the n measurements of a recording, so divide by n = 10 / (Delta/tau) (the number
# of acquisition windows; matches num_samples in figure_3_paper.Rmd). Then the mean
# standardized residual -> 0 (A1) and the mean SQUARED residual -> 1 (A2) when the
# predictive variance is right. Facet by algorithm so NR/NMR don't crush R/MR/IR.
resid <- d_mean %>%
  filter(component %in% c("Moment_statistics_Sum_r_std",
                          "Moment_statistics_Sum_r2_std"),
         statistic == "mean", !is.na(mean_NA)) %>%
  mutate(
    # measurements per recording = 10 / (Delta/tau); exactly the num_samples vector
    # in figure_3_paper.Rmd (1.00->10, 0.5->20, ... 0.01->1000)
    n_samples  = round(10 / interval_in_tau),
    scaled     = mean_NA        / n_samples,
    scaled_lo  = quantile_0.025 / n_samples,        # 95% bootstrap CI, same scaling
    scaled_hi  = quantile_0.975 / n_samples)

# r (null 0): SYMMETRIC LINEAR scale around 0 (geom_blank mirror forces |y|<=ext)
resid_r <- resid %>% filter(component == "Moment_statistics_Sum_r_std") %>%
  mutate(ext = pmax(abs(scaled), abs(scaled_lo), abs(scaled_hi), na.rm = TRUE))
# r^2 (null 1): SYMMETRIC LOG scale around 1 (mirror is the reciprocal)
resid_r2 <- resid %>% filter(component == "Moment_statistics_Sum_r2_std") %>%
  mutate(extlog = pmax(abs(log10(scaled)), abs(log10(scaled_lo)),
                       abs(log10(scaled_hi)), na.rm = TRUE))

gA1 <- ggplot(resid_r, aes(interval_in_tau, scaled, colour = factor(Num_ch), group = Num_ch)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "#009E73", linewidth = .4) +
  geom_blank(aes(y = ext)) + geom_blank(aes(y = -ext)) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = scaled_lo, ymax = scaled_hi), alpha = .5) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ algo, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_colour_viridis_d(name = "N_ch", end = .9) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "mean residual\nr  (null 0)",
       title = "A  Residual diagnostics per measurement (sum / n,  n = 10/(Delta/tau); bars = 95% bootstrap CI)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

gA2 <- ggplot(resid_r2, aes(interval_in_tau, scaled, colour = factor(Num_ch), group = Num_ch)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "#009E73", linewidth = .4) +
  geom_blank(aes(y = 10^extlog)) + geom_blank(aes(y = 10^(-extlog))) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = scaled_lo, ymax = scaled_hi), alpha = .5) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ algo, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10() + scale_colour_viridis_d(name = "N_ch", end = .9) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta/tau[min]), y = "mean sq residual\n r^2  (null 1, log)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
gA1 / gA2
```

```{r panelB-score, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 3}
# STANDARDIZED score = mean score / sqrt(Fisher diagonal). The raw sum scales with
# N_ch (it crushes small-N_ch curves within a panel); the standardized score is
# O(1) and N_ch-comparable, ~0 under correct specification. Facet by algorithm.
fish_diag <- d_mean %>%
  filter(component == "Moment_statistics_Sum_Gaussian_Fisher_Information",
         statistic == "mean", param_index == param_col, param_index %in% c(0, 1),
         !is.na(mean_NA)) %>%
  transmute(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, fisher = mean_NA)

score_z <- d_mean %>%
  filter(component == "Moment_statistics_Sum_dlogL", statistic == "mean",
         param_name %in% KIN, !is.na(mean_NA)) %>%
  transmute(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, param_name, score = mean_NA,
            q_lo = quantile_0.025, q_hi = quantile_0.975) %>%       # 95% bootstrap CI
  left_join(fish_diag, by = c("algo","Num_ch","interval_in_tau","param_index")) %>%
  mutate(z = score / sqrt(fisher),
         z_lo = q_lo / sqrt(fisher), z_hi = q_hi / sqrt(fisher),
         ext = pmax(abs(z), abs(z_lo), abs(z_hi), na.rm = TRUE))  # symmetric around 0

gB <- ggplot(score_z, aes(interval_in_tau, z, colour = factor(Num_ch),
                          linetype = param_name,
                          group = interaction(Num_ch, param_name))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey40", linewidth = .3) +
  geom_blank(aes(y = ext)) + geom_blank(aes(y = -ext)) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = z_lo, ymax = z_hi), alpha = .4) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ algo, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_colour_viridis_d(name = "N_ch", end = .9) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(on = "solid", off = "22"), name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta/tau[min]), y = "standardized score\n(score / sqrt Fisher)",
       title = "B  Expected score ~ 0 at the true parameters (standardized)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
gB
```

```{r panelC-bias}
# bootstrap of the pooled MLE -> bias vs truth, idioms from figure_3_paper.Rmd
boot_param_probit <- function(x, B = 100, cis = c(0.025,0.16,0.5,0.84,0.975)) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]; n <- length(x)
  if (n < 1) return(tibble(mean_NA=NA_real_, quantile_0.025=NA_real_,
    quantile_0.16=NA_real_, quantile_0.5=NA_real_, quantile_0.84=NA_real_,
    quantile_0.975=NA_real_, n_units=0L, theta_bar=NA_real_))
  reps <- colMeans(matrix(sample(x, n * B, replace = TRUE), nrow = n))
  q <- quantile(reps, cis, type = 6, names = FALSE)
  tibble(mean_NA = mean(reps), quantile_0.025 = q[1], quantile_0.16 = q[2],
         quantile_0.5 = q[3], quantile_0.84 = q[4], quantile_0.975 = q[5],
         n_units = n, theta_bar = mean(x))
}
theta_sim_of <- function(param_name, Num_ch) dplyr::case_when(
  param_name == "on" ~ log10(10), param_name == "off" ~ log10(100),
  param_name == "unitary_current" ~ log10(1), param_name == "Current_Noise" ~ log10(1e-4),
  param_name == "Current_Baseline" ~ log10(1), param_name == "Num_ch_mean" ~ log10(Num_ch),
  TRUE ~ NA_real_)

# CONVERGENCE FILTER. The Gauss-Newton optimiser caps at 100 iterations;
# Convergence_Status_Code == 0 means converged, == 3 means it hit the cap without
# converging. NR and NMR saturate the cap at long intervals (Δ/τ >= 0.2): there the
# measured θ̄ is garbage and does NOT match the predicted bias. We keep only
# converged fits and drop cells where < 50% of fits converged (annotated below).
conv <- d %>%
  filter(component == "Convergence_Status_Code") %>%
  transmute(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, group_size, sample_index, conv = value) %>%
  distinct()

conv_frac <- conv %>%
  filter(group_size == BIAS_GROUP) %>%
  group_by(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau) %>%
  summarise(frac_conv = mean(conv == 0), .groups = "drop")

set.seed(1)
mle_bias <- d %>%
  filter(component == "Model_Parameters_Hat", statistic == "value",
         group_size == BIAS_GROUP, param_name %in% PARAMS_C) %>%
  left_join(conv, by = c("algo","Num_ch","interval_in_tau","group_size","sample_index")) %>%
  filter(conv == 0) %>%                                  # keep converged fits only
  group_by(Num_ch, algo, interval_in_tau, param_name) %>%
  group_modify(~ boot_param_probit(.x$value)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(conv_frac, by = c("algo","Num_ch","interval_in_tau")) %>%
  mutate(theta_sim = theta_sim_of(param_name, Num_ch),    # plot the bootstrap median
         bias  = quantile_0.5   - theta_sim,
         lo16  = quantile_0.16  - theta_sim, hi84  = quantile_0.84  - theta_sim,
         lo025 = quantile_0.025 - theta_sim, hi975 = quantile_0.975 - theta_sim,
         ext   = pmax(abs(bias), abs(lo025), abs(hi975), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%  # symmetric around 0
  filter(frac_conv >= 0.5)                                # drop non-converged regimes (all N_ch kept)

# PREDICTED bias = Distortion-Induced Bias = (numerical FD-Fisher)^-1 * score, at
# theta_sim. The FD-Fisher goes near-singular in scattered cells -> the DIB blows up;
# drop those with Luciano's filters (N_ch>10 and a sane bootstrap CI), NOT the
# measured bias, where every N_ch is real (incl. the N_ch=10 non-normality signal).
dib <- d_mean %>%
  filter(component == "Likelihood_Distortion_Induced_Bias", statistic == "value",
         param_name %in% PARAMS_C, Num_ch > 10,
         quantile_0.975 < 50, quantile_0.025 > -50) %>%
  transmute(algo, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name, bias = quantile_0.5)
```

```{r panelC-plot, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 6}
# one panel per ALGORITHM (param in rows), colour by N_ch. The bias is NOT
# intensive: it carries real N_ch structure (e.g. unitary_current is non-monotonic
# in N_ch from the departure from normality), which is why colouring by N_ch matters.
# measured bias = points + 68% (thick) and 95% (thin) bootstrap CI; the dashed line
# is the predicted DIB where the FD-Fisher is well-conditioned.
gC <- ggplot(mapping = aes(interval_in_tau, bias, colour = factor(Num_ch))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey40", linewidth = .3) +
  # symmetric around 0, sized by the MEASURED bias (the DIB overshoot may run off-panel)
  geom_blank(data = mle_bias, aes(y = ext)) +
  geom_blank(data = mle_bias, aes(y = -ext)) +
  geom_linerange(data = mle_bias, aes(ymin = lo025, ymax = hi975), alpha = .3) +
  geom_linerange(data = mle_bias, aes(ymin = lo16, ymax = hi84),
                 linewidth = .9, alpha = .6) +
  geom_point(data = mle_bias, size = 1) +
  geom_line(data = dib, aes(group = Num_ch), linetype = "22") +
  facet_grid(param_name ~ algo, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = "N_ch", end = .9) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta/tau[min]), y = "bias (log10)",
       title = "C  Measured bias (points, 68%/95% bootstrap CI) vs predicted DIB (dashed)",
       subtitle = "one panel per algorithm; measured keeps every N_ch; DIB shown only where the numerical FD-Fisher is well-conditioned") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
gC
```

```{r compose, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 17}
fig3 <- gA1 / gA2 / gB / gC +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 1, 2.6), guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
fig3
ggsave("Figure_3.pdf", fig3, width = 12, height = 17)
```
